title: Risky Refactor
description: A refactor benchmark with high-risk operations, validation tasks, and approval pressure.
goal: Show that risk and approval metadata survive optimizer reporting and persistence.
expected_constraints: Validation and rollback planning appear before risky changes when dependencies allow.
Risk is reported without claiming the task is automatically safe.
notes: Risk changes total utility and approval records even when dependency order is straightforward.

tasks.title: Map refactor surface
tasks.description: Identify modules, tests, and rollback points before editing.

tasks.title: Design rollback plan
tasks.description: Prepare a rollback strategy for a shared runtime refactor.

tasks.title: Change runtime boundary
tasks.description: Apply a shared runtime refactor that could break multiple commands.

tasks.title: Run regression suite
tasks.description: Run validation after the high-risk refactor.

context_candidates.content: High-risk refactors need rollback notes before edits.
The runtime boundary is used by CLI and persistence flows.
An old brainstorming thread with marginally relevant notes.
